video_title: Kelly O’s Haluski Has Guy Hallucinating 🌀| Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Kelly O's
restaurants.guy_intro: But that didn't stop one former waitress here in Pittsburgh, Kelly O'Connor, who took out a small loan and bought a little joint in a strip mall and turned it into a neighborhood favorite. Gotta check out Kelly O's.

dishes.description: Noodles, onions, cabbage, and bacon, cooked with butter, garlic salt, and Romano cheese.
dishes.guy_response: I'm having a haloski nation. That's one of the surprise dishes I've had on Drupal D. Mom, I found a new haloski. Don't be mad. She's going to spank your kabooski when she finds out you love this haloski.

restaurants.name: Eva's
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Syracuse, New York, right on the city line and about a mile and a half from the fairgrounds to check out a joint that's run by a Polish family that immigrated to the States in 88. This is Eva's.

dishes.description: Potato pancakes made from grated potatoes, onion, egg, pepper, spices, salt, flour, fried until crisp. Served with a Hungarian sauce made from ground beef, onion, peppers, garlic, diced tomato, tomato puree, paprika, cayenne, chili flake, pepper, salt, and spices. Topped with sour cream.
dishes.guy_response: That's really nice comfort food. Potato pancake. Nice and crisp outside. Tender on the inside. And then there's gravy. A little bit of spice to it. Oh, great. It's good.

restaurants.name: Cafe Polonia
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in the south side of Boston in an area known as the Polish Triangle. Now, I'm here to check out exactly what you'd expect, a Polish restaurant. But not just any Polish restaurant. We're talking about one of the most real deal, authentic Polish restaurants you'll find in Massachusetts. This is Cafe Polonia.

dishes.description: Pierogis filled with a mixture of ground pork shoulder and other meat, cooked with celery, parsley, leeks, carrots, bay leaves, allspice, granulated garlic, granulated onions, and Vegetta spices. Served with sour cream, caramelized onions, and bacon bits.
dishes.guy_response: Very light, very tender. When people say, oh, pierogi, they've had frozen, not handmade pierogis. And if you try this, it'll change the way you think of them.

restaurants.name: The Rally Inn
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Cleveland. Happens to be a joint that's serving Cleveland comfort food. I got to check it out. This is the Rally Inn.

dishes.description: A Cleveland spin on Hawaiian Locomoco, featuring four-cheese pierogies, an eight-ounce ground beef patty mixed with green peppers, red peppers, red onions, and garlic, topped with brown pepper gravy (made with butter, flour, black pepper, salt, onion powder, garlic powder, chicken base, beef base, cornstarch slurry), and fried red onion frazzles. Served with eggs over easy.
dishes.guy_response: Dude, it just lights out. Locamoski. The pierogies are like butter. The meat has a little bit of spice.

restaurants.name: Mom Mom's Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: So we're here in Bridesburg, right in the northeast side of Philadelphia, to check out a joint where they're using grandma's recipes to make it the real deal. This is Mom Mom's Kitchen.

dishes.description: Pierogis made with a dough of sour cream, melted butter, oil, whole eggs, egg yolks, AP flour, and kosher salt. Filled with thinly sliced ribeye and Cooper Sharp American cheese. Cooked in water, then seared on a flat-top grill. Served with black pepper ketchup, caramelized onions, and scallions.
dishes.guy_response: I've had a lot of pierogis on Triple D, and I've had a lot of pierogis just in regular life. That pierogi dough with the sour cream and the whole barnyard of chickens, it's silky, luxurious, melts in your mouth. Wow! It's absolutely wrecking regular cheese steaks for me right now. And you guys made this up. Don't get me wrong. I like a little sauce. I used to have that little acid, little sweet. Dynamite, dynamite. I mean, this isn't just like, hey, my grandmother was Polish. This is like a legitimate, groundbreaking destination dish.
